verses.text: On the eighth day Moses called Aaron and his sons. To Aaron: a calf for sin, a ram for burnt. Tell Israel: a kid for sin, a calf and lamb for burnt; a bullock and ram for peace; meat offering with oil — today the LORD will appear. They brought all to the tabernacle. Moses: the glory shall appear. Aaron, atone for thyself and the people. Guidance: The eighth day — a new beginning after the week. Sin first, burnt second, peace third — sins covered, life given, fellowship enjoyed. The pattern hasn't changed.

On the eighth day Moses called Aaron and his sons. To Aaron: a calf for sin, a ram for burnt. Tell Israel: a kid for sin, a calf and lamb for burnt; a bullock and ram for peace; meat offering with oil — today the LORD will appear. They brought all to the tabernacle. Moses: the glory shall appear. Aaron, atone for thyself and the people. Guidance: The eighth day — a new beginning after the week. Sin first, burnt second, peace third — sins covered, life given, fellowship enjoyed. The pattern hasn't changed.

Aaron went to the altar, slew the calf of sin offering for himself. His sons brought him the blood; he dipped his finger, put on the altar's horns, poured the rest at the base. Burnt fat, kidneys, caul on the altar. Flesh and hide burned outside the camp. Guidance: Aaron's first act as priest is to atone for himself. Hebrews 5 reads this carefully: every priest needs his own sin offering — except Jesus, who needed none. The lesson of Aaron is the proof of Christ.

Burnt fat, kidneys, caul on the altar. Flesh and hide burned outside the camp. Guidance: Aaron's first act as priest is to atone for himself. Hebrews 5 reads this carefully: every priest needs his own sin offering — except Jesus, who needed none. The lesson of Aaron is the proof of Christ.

Burnt fat, kidneys, caul on the altar. Flesh and hide burned outside the camp. Guidance: Aaron's first act as priest is to atone for himself. Hebrews 5 reads this carefully: every priest needs his own sin offering — except Jesus, who needed none. The lesson of Aaron is the proof of Christ.

He slew the burnt offering, his sons brought him blood; he sprinkled it round the altar. Brought him the pieces and the head; he burnt them on the altar. Washed the inwards and legs; burnt them with the burnt offering on the altar. Guidance: After his sin is covered, Aaron offers himself wholly. The burnt offering rises in smoke — the priest's life given completely. You cannot give what you have not first surrendered.

The burnt offering rises in smoke — the priest's life given completely. You cannot give what you have not first surrendered.

He slew the burnt offering, his sons brought him blood; he sprinkled it round the altar. Brought him the pieces and the head; he burnt them on the altar. Washed the inwards and legs; burnt them with the burnt offering on the altar. Guidance: After his sin is covered, Aaron offers himself wholly. The burnt offering rises in smoke — the priest's life given completely. You cannot give what you have not first surrendered.

Brought the people's offering: the kid for sin offering of the people — slew, offered as the first. Brought the burnt offering, offered according to the manner. Brought the meat offering, took a handful, burnt on the altar beside the morning's burnt sacrifice. Guidance: Now Aaron turns from himself to the people. Same order, same blood, same fire — but now for them. The priest's purpose is finally fulfilled: standing between the people and the altar.

Brought the people's offering: the kid for sin offering of the people — slew, offered as the first. Brought the burnt offering, offered according to the manner. Brought the meat offering, took a handful, burnt on the altar beside the morning's burnt sacrifice. Guidance: Now Aaron turns from himself to the people. Same order, same blood, same fire — but now for them. The priest's purpose is finally fulfilled: standing between the people and the altar.

Brought the people's offering: the kid for sin offering of the people — slew, offered as the first. Brought the burnt offering, offered according to the manner. Brought the meat offering, took a handful, burnt on the altar beside the morning's burnt sacrifice. Guidance: Now Aaron turns from himself to the people. Same order, same blood, same fire — but now for them. The priest's purpose is finally fulfilled: standing between the people and the altar.

Slew the bullock and ram for the people's peace offering. Sons brought blood; sprinkled round the altar. Fat — rump, inwards, kidneys, caul above liver — laid on breasts; burnt on the altar. Breasts and right shoulder Aaron waved before the LORD. Guidance: Sin atoned, life given, now fellowship celebrated. The peace offering ends in a shared meal. God's table is open after the blood has been spilled. Every altar leads home to a feast.

Breasts and right shoulder Aaron waved before the LORD. Guidance: Sin atoned, life given, now fellowship celebrated. The peace offering ends in a shared meal. Aaron lifted his hand, blessed the people, came down. Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle, came out, blessed the people. The glory of the LORD appeared. Fire came out from before the LORD, consumed the burnt offering. The people shouted, fell on their faces. Guidance: God's fire fell on the offering — accepted. The people shouted and fell. This is what Sinai promised: God dwelling in the midst of His people, His glory visible, His acceptance unmistakable.
